title: eriocoma wallowaensis
intro: Eriocoma wallowaensis, also known as Wallowa needle grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Native to the Wallowa Mountains in Oregon, USA, it has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Traditionally, Wallowa needle grass is prepared by drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
